items.project_definition: A presentation layer interface used to expose business logic externally, kept strictly isolated from data access and core services.
The four foundational persistent storage mutation primitives that require mandatory prior unit testing.
The final step of the development sequence ensuring the root markdown file and function metadata are current.
The phase of writing the absolute minimum source code necessary to satisfy a failing test case.
The interaction layer restricted to a command-line environment for this system.
The primary programming tool specified as version 3.10 or higher.
A deprecated legacy system whose principles were entirely replaced during the version 1.0.0 transition.
The required runtime environment for all source code execution.
The primary technical project documentation file that must be updated before a task is considered complete.
The mandatory initial phase where functional requirements and design contracts are defined in the specified folder.
The local persistence mechanism utilizing a structured file-based database.
A software development methodology where tests are authored before the functional logic to drive the design.
The development step of creating a failing test case specifically for a mutation operation before writing logic.
The quality assurance process utilizing the pytest framework for all suites.
The presentation layer that must remain strictly isolated from the service and repository layers.
The process of executing the entire suite of tests to ensure no regressions were introduced.
A design principle prohibiting the addition of features or complexity until a direct requirement is proven.
